location.office_time_required: at least three days per week

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in program management required
Base salary range $189,000-$225,750 in San Francisco, consistent with Principal/Staff IC
Partners directly with execs and senior leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 8+ years of experience in program management with a track record of leading large, complex, cross-functional programs in a fast paced AI/SaaS B2B environment with an evolving strategy
Proven experience working directly with senior level leadership, you're comfortable engaging with and influencing senior executives and leadership to get to the right outcome
High ownership and agency, with the ability to manage multiple high-profile, high-visibility programs simultaneously in a fast-paced environment
A strategic thinker who connects execution to company strategy, spots gaps across programs, and understands how mission, product, and financial plan tie together
You are solutions oriented and proactive when it comes to taking action to solve problems and mitigate risks
You have strong facilitation and stakeholder management skills with a track record of bringing together senior-level viewpoints and driving alignment
You're an excellent self-starter with a track record of seamless execution on complex programs with minimal guidance
You've built and significantly improved program management practices, frameworks, or ways of working at a previous company
Excellent communicator who can flex between an exec room and a cross-functional working group, adapting style and depth to the audience
Experience mentoring or developing others, whether direct reports or peers - you make the people around you better

scope.surface: Strategic Program Management team; cross-company highest-priority programs at Fin (Intercom)
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of high-priority company objectives
core_responsibilities: Partner directly with execs and senior leadership to define programs of work that enable Fin to hit its highest priority company objectives
Take a holistic view across programs, connecting the dots back to company strategy and surfacing gaps, misalignment, or opportunities to streamline
Take ambiguous, high-stakes problems and drive them to clarity - defining the right structure, roles, and decision-making frameworks, often from scratch
Drive cross-functional decision-making by identifying the right stakeholders, surfacing the information they need and recommendations
Proactively identify program risks and anticipate change before it happens, driving action to course-correct and maintain momentum
Set and raise the bar for how program management works at Fin - creating new frameworks, challenging existing processes that aren't working, and feeding learnings back into how the team operates
Hold people accountable at every level, from workstream drivers to exec sponsors, ensuring progress and ownership across complex, cross-functional programs
Mentor and develop teammates, making the people around you better and faster
Evangelize program management across the company - shaping how other teams and orgs think about execution, collaboration, and ways of working

compensation.equity_form: Restricted Stock Units (RSUs); also eligible for corporate bonus program

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($189,000-$225,750 base) for San Francisco Bay Area
RSUs and bonus program mentioned as additional compensation
Claude Code and best-in-class AI tools access is a differentiator for practitioners
Clear reporting structure (Senior Manager, Strategic Program Management)
Specific hybrid policy articulated (3 days/week in-office)
Role has clear seniority framing ('senior individual contributor') reducing ambiguity
red_flags: No mention of visa sponsorship; unclear for international candidates
Hybrid policy requires 3 days/week in San Francisco office — eliminates remote-only candidates
No nice-to-haves listed, making it harder to gauge what differentiates candidates
JD uses 'Fin' and 'Intercom' interchangeably, which may cause branding/organizational confusion for candidates researching the role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level inferred from compensation range, 'senior individual contributor' label, and years requirement
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.visa_sponsorship inferred as unclear (not addressed in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential inferred from explicit statement that range applies to San Francisco Bay Area candidates only
ambiguities: JD uses both 'Fin' and 'Intercom' as company names — Fin appears to be the rebranded product/company name while Intercom is the ATS entity; the role is posted under Intercom's ATS but the JD body consistently refers to the company as 'Fin'
No nice-to-haves are explicitly listed in the JD; all requirements are presented as unconditional, so all were classified as must-haves
